rxn_class_summary: Anode-material-tuned electrochemical oxidative [4+2] annulation or dioxygenation of olefins with hydroxamic acids to give 3,4-dihydro-1H-benzo[c][1,2]oxazines or 1,2-dioxygenated alkenes
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of hydroxamic acid (and deprotonation) generates an O-centered amidoxyl radical; electrode material (RVC vs C felt) dictates whether the intermediate C-radical is further oxidized to a cation (RVC, annulation) or trapped by a second amidoxyl radical (C felt, dioxygenation). For indole substrates, both hydroxamic acid and indole are simultaneously anodically oxidized to amidoxyl radical and indole radical cation respectively.

key_intermediates.name: C-centered radical II from amidoxyl addition to olefin
benzylic/alkyl carbocation III (after further anodic oxidation on RVC)
indole radical cation IV (for N-pivaloyl indole substrates)
radical-radical-coupling cation V (amidoxyl + indole radical cation)
MNP-trapped aminoxyl adduct Rc (spin-trap nitroxide of C-radical)

electron_flow: Anode performs single-electron oxidation of the hydroxamic acid; after -H+ the resulting amidoxyl O-radical adds to the olefin to give a C-radical. On RVC the C-radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that undergoes intramolecular electrophilic substitution (IES) onto the N-aryl ring giving the benzo[c][1,2]oxazine; on C felt, the C-radical is instead intercepted by a second amidoxyl radical giving the 1,2-dioxygenated product. Cathode reduces protons to H2.
rate_determining_step: Not the C-H cleavage of the phenyl moiety of the hydroxamic acid (KIE ≈ 1.0 with styrene); likely the amidoxyl radical addition / anodic oxidation step

probe_reagents.name: a1-D5 (deuterated hydroxamic acid) competition KIE with styrene b1
probe_reagents.observation: KIE = 1.0; phenyl C-H cleavage of hydroxamic acid is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: a1-D5 competition KIE with norbornene b22
a1-D5 competition KIE with N-pivaloyl indole b24
2-methyl-2-nitrosopropane (MNP)
probe_reagents.observation: Spin-trapped a transient C-centered radical giving sextuple EPR signal of aminoxyl Rc (aN=15.31 G, aHβ=3.46 G, g=2.0060), confirming amidoxyl addition onto styrene

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of a1, b1, b22, b24
probe_reagents.observation: a1 oxidized at 1.32 V; b1 and b22 not oxidized within 2 V (only a1 oxidized); b24 oxidized at 1.58 V (both a1 and b24 oxidized simultaneously); reductive peak of amidoxyl radical at 0.78 V disappears upon adding b1 or b24, indicating interception by olefin/indole

probe_reagents.name: Constant-potential electrolysis at +1.32 V (a1 + b1, undivided RVC/Pt cell)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave annulation product c1 (35%) and dioxygenation product d1 (20%), confirming both pathways branch from the same C-radical

probe_reagents.name: EPR of electrolyzed a1
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet signal (aN=8.03 G, g=2.0058) assigned to metastable amidoxyl radical; signal decreases on adding styrene

notes: Anode-material selectivity is rationalized as easier further oxidation of the C-radical to cation on RVC than on C felt; on C felt the C-radical instead couples with a second amidoxyl radical. For indole substrate b24, because its oxidation potential is close to that of a1, a radical-radical-coupling (RCC) pathway between amidoxyl radical and indole radical cation V is proposed rather than (or in addition to) amidoxyl radical addition followed by oxidation. Indole was unsuitable for dioxygenation. The MNP spin-adduct Rc is an off-pathway diagnostic species, not a productive intermediate.
